channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Xbox Partner Preview March 2026 - All the News and Reveals
item.content:encoded:
Today, Xbox held its latest Partner Preview showcase, and it contained about 30 minutes of info for upcoming games from third-party developers and publishers. Here's everything that was shown!

Nacon kicks off with Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish

First up, we got to see the announcement trailer for Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish, a first-person action RPG from Nacon and RoboCop: Rogue City developer Teyon. The game is coming to PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S in summer, and you can read more about it here.

Wuthering Waves hits Xbox in July

Gacha action RPG Wuthering Waves, which definitely isn't inspired by HoYoverse's suite of similar games, is coming to Xbox Series X|S in July, and we got to see a trailer showing off some of its admittedly very varied gameplay.

The Expanse: Osiris Reborn wants that Mass Effect audience

Owlcat's The Expanse: Osiris Reborn is up next, and we got to see a little of everything in the trailer; some third-person combat (very reminiscent of Mass Effect), a couple of dialogue trees, and some vehicular space exploration for good measure. Spring 2027 is when you can check this one out, and a closed beta is coming in April.

Grave Seasons is a cozy game with a twist

If you're a little sick of all of your cozy games being just a bit too genteel (no shame), then Grave Seasons could be for you. It's got all the usual stuff - think farming, exploration, NPC romance - but there's also something much creepier going on. This one's coming on August 14th, and a demo will be available soon, too.

Serious Sam: Shatterverse has plenty of co-op mayhem to offer

Next up, it's Serious Sam: Shatterverse, Devolver and Behaviour Interactive's spin of the co-op shooter wheel. It combines Serious Sam's signature horde-based combat with a new-era Painkiller-style emphasis on multiplayer, and it's launching this year.

Stranger Than Heaven has five eras and five cities for you

Ryu Ga Gotoku's Stranger Than Heaven is next, and it looks like typical Like a Dragon-style action fare, albeit set in a hugely ambitious five-city and five-era span. The jazz soundtrack seems pretty awesome, too. We'll get more info on this one on May 6th, which could also be when we get that long-awaited release date (which wasn't announced today).

Super Meat Boy 3D launches next week!

Sluggerfly and Team Meat's 3D take on Super Meat Boy is next, and it's coming next week, bringing the series' anarchic brand of super-difficult masocore platforming to the 3D realm. March 31st is that all-important release date.

Forever Ago has that Annapurna charm

It's an Annapurna Interactive game next, and if you hadn't told me Forever Ago was made by the publisher, I'd have pegged Annapurna for this gentle blend of adventure-style exploration and poignant storytelling anyway. Fall 2026 for this.

Ascend to Zero offers fast-paced fun

Next up, we got a look at Ascend to Zero, which looks to be an ultra-fast-paced action game (and probably a roguelite) in which you only have a certain amount of time to navigate a series of isometric rooms while killing enemies and dodging traps. It launches on July 13th.

Dispatch is heading to Xbox

If you haven't had your chance to check out AdHoc's Dispatch yet, then perhaps its Xbox release may convince you. It's hitting Xbox platforms in summer.

Rebellion reveals Alien Deathstorm

Our next game is Alien Deathstorm, and it seems pretty aptly-named; we get to see a lot of storms courtesy of some pretty gnarly-looking wind, as well as a lot of aliens dying in first-person shooter combat. You can play this one in 2027.

Frog Sqwad is coming to Xbox

Do you like physics-based multiplayer fun? How about weirdly gloopy ASMR-style voices? Frog Sqwad has both, and it's heading to Xbox in June.

Bluey's Happy Snaps lets you take lots of, well, happy snaps

Bluey's Happy Snaps is next, and it's a photography game that kind of looks like what would happen if you let the Toem guys loose in the world of Bluey. It arrives in fall.

Stalker 2 is getting a new expansion

GSC Game World showed off Stalker 2: Cost of Hope next, and it's a big expansion for the base game that's coming in summer. Expect new run-down areas to explore, lots more story to experience, and plenty of gear to loot.

Moosa: Dirty Fate has some serious hack-and-slash cred

If you're on the hunt for an Onimusha-style hack-and-slash, then Moosa: Dirty Fate could be the game for you, despite its weird name. It's coming sometime next year and bringing a story that looks like it revolves around the age-old revenge chestnut with it.

The Eternal Life of Goldman lands sometime this year

Next up, it's a Cuphead-style platformer starring an old man who appears, to quote the trailer, to have become so old that he "simply forget[s] to die". This one looks like a lot of fun; it's launching in 2026, and a demo is available to try out today.

Vaunted tells the tale of an expedition gone wrong

Something a little more colorful now with Vaunted, an action-RPG with turn-based elements that tells the story of a horribly botched expedition. You'll be able to find out just how botched when it launches next year.

Hades 2 comes to Xbox in April

Still need to catch up with Supergiant's roguelite sequel Hades 2? You'll get your chance to do just that when it hits Xbox (and PS5) on April 14th.

Artificial Detective is a cyberpunk thriller with a robo-protagonist

We close out the show with Artificial Detective, a third-person action-adventure game that tasks your robotic protagonist with finding out the truth behind a malevolent-seeming rogue technology. It's coming in 2027.

That's your lot! Stay tuned for more on everything that was revealed during today's showcase.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
